title: Add ckBTC/ckETH/ckUSDC glyph symbols across token UI

requirements.summary: Extend token configuration with a human-friendly glyph for ckBTC, ckETH, and ckUSDC and provide an accessor by token key.
acceptanceCriteria: `frontend/src/config/tokens.ts` includes a new field on token configs (or a dedicated mapping) that provides a symbol glyph for ckBTC, ckETH, and ckUSDC.
There is a simple accessor (e.g., via `getTokenConfig` or a new helper) that the UI can use to fetch the glyph for a given token key.
Existing token configuration checks and existing properties (name, symbol, ledgerCanisterId, decimals) continue to work without regressions.

file_operations.description: Add a distinct presentational glyph for ckBTC/ckETH/ckUSDC (either as a new `glyph` field on `TokenConfig` and each `TOKENS` entry, or as a dedicated `TOKEN_GLYPHS` mapping). Export a small helper (e.g., `getTokenGlyph(tokenKey: string): string`) that safely returns the configured glyph (with a sensible fallback) without changing existing config validation behavior.

requirements.summary: Show configured glyphs alongside token tickers in the Dashboard Tokens section.
acceptanceCriteria: In `frontend/src/components/dashboard/TokensSection.tsx`, each token row/card shows both the token ticker (ckBTC/ckETH/ckUSDC) and the configured glyph from the token config.
The glyph display is purely presentational (no new backend calls) and renders correctly in both light and dark themes.

file_operations.description: Import the new glyph accessor from `@/config/tokens` and render the glyph next to the token ticker in the token header area for each token card/row, using existing Tailwind theme tokens/classes so it remains legible in light and dark modes. Keep the change presentational only (no new data fetching).

requirements.summary: Use token glyphs consistently in send/receive panel headings, button text, and success messages (English UI text).
acceptanceCriteria: `frontend/src/components/dashboard/TokenReceivePanel.tsx` displays the token glyph alongside the token ticker in the main heading (e.g., "Your ₿ ckBTC Receiving Address" or similar).
`frontend/src/components/dashboard/TokenSendPanel.tsx` includes the glyph alongside the token ticker in the primary action button label and the success toast message.
All existing validation and disabled-state behavior remains unchanged.

file_operations.description: Import the glyph accessor from `@/config/tokens` and update the main heading text to include the glyph alongside the token ticker (English phrasing). Keep all copy-to-clipboard and empty-state behavior unchanged.

Import the glyph accessor from `@/config/tokens` and include the glyph alongside the token ticker in (1) the primary send button label and (2) the success toast message. Preserve existing validation, disabled-state, and placeholder send logic behavior.
